question: Decarboxylation of all six possible forms of diaminobenzoic acids <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>COOH</mi></math> yields three products <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. Three acids give a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>', two acids gives a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>' and one acid give a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>'. The melting point of product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>' is
solution:
C is a para isomer.

The melting point of para-isomer is comparatively higher than the corresponding  ortho and meta-isomers , because of more symmetrical nature; it fits into crystal lattice more readily and so, close packing of molecules in crystal lattice increases the intermolecular force of attraction between molecules.

Hence C has highest melting point that is 142 °C

solution:
This reaction is known as HVZ reaction.

Treatment with bromine and a catalytic amount of phosphorus leads to the selective α-bromination of carboxylic acids.

subject: Chemistry
q: The increasing order of the acidity of the following carboxylic acids is:
question: The increasing order of the acidity of the following carboxylic acids is:
solution: The increasing order of the acidity of the carboxylic acids is III $ < $ II $ < $ IV $ < $ I. In aromatic acids, electron withdrawing groups like $\mathrm{Cl},-\mathrm{CN},-\mathrm{NO}_2$ increases the acidity, whereas electron releasing groups like $\mathrm{CH}_3,-\mathrm{OH},-\mathrm{OCH}_3,-\mathrm{NH}_2$ decreases the acidity.

q: Compound (A), <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>8</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>9</mn></msub><mi>Cl</mi></math>, gives a white precipitate when warmed with alcoholic <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>AgNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math><sub>.</sub> Oxidation of (A) gives an acid (B), <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>8</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>. (B) easily forms anhydride on heating. Identify the compound (A).
question: Compound (A), <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>8</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>9</mn></msub><mi>Cl</mi></math>, gives a white precipitate when warmed with alcoholic <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>AgNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math><sub>.</sub> Oxidation of (A) gives an acid (B), <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>8</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>. (B) easily forms anhydride on heating. Identify the compound (A).
solution: Compound (A) gives a white precipitate when warmed with alcoholic AgNO3means it contain a removable chloride atom(Benzylic position). Oxidation of (A) gives an acid (B), C8H6O4. (B) easily forms anhydride on heating. Means both acid should be ortho position of each other.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Two statements are given belwo :

Statement I : The melting point of monocarboxylic acid with even number of carbon atoms is higher than that of with odd number of carbon atoms acid immediately below and above it in the series.

Statement II : The solubility of monocarboxylic acids in water decreases with increase in molar mass.

Choose the most appropriate option :
question:
Two statements are given belwo :

Statement I : The melting point of monocarboxylic acid with even number of carbon atoms is higher than that of with odd number of carbon atoms acid immediately below and above it in the series.

Statement II : The solubility of monocarboxylic acids in water decreases with increase in molar mass.

Choose the most appropriate option :
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
solution:
The melting point of carboxylic acid has no trend as we can observe from the following data of metling point.

Methanoic acid =8 °C, Ethanoic acid =17 °C, Propanoic acid =2 °C, Butanoic acid =-6 °C, Pentonoic acid =-35 °C, Hexonic acid =-20 °C, Heptanoic acid =-8 °C, Octanoic acid =17 °C acid. Water solubility of carboxylic acid decreases as we increase the molar mass & hence hydrophobic part of the chain.
